# NCB-0001 — Batch Manifest

## Registry Summary

**Canonical Person Registry (Animalia, pre-batch):**

- **Dossiers (2):** CHAR-0001 Vespera Nightwhisper (cat-folk, Mudbrook bard-contractor); CHAR-0002 "Threadscar" Melissa (human, Mudbrook named-veteran contractor).
- **Diegetic-artifact authors (2, same persons as dossiers):** DA-0001 Vespera (travelogue to Brinewick journal); DA-0002 Melissa (Harrowgate after-action report).
- **Offstage-gravity persons:** Aldous (Mudbrook ceramic-extractor); Rill (17, north-road waystation apprentice); Bertram "the Muddy" (Mudbrook armorer); Registrar Copperplate (Mudbrook Municipal Aid Registry); Tulen/Auveth (dead Harrowgate bandit-hunters); Gresh/Karn (surviving contractors); historical kiln-explosion victims (Tenth-Loom potter's family; Long Quay forge-master; Fen-Edge scholar).
- **PA adjudications (16):** all concern canon facts, not persons; no historically-salient figures added to Registry.

**Essence-profile coverage:** 7-layer profiles derived for all 13 Registry entries (world-position / function / pressure / access / epistemic / thematic / voice).

## Constellation + Mosaic Audit

**Constellation view (direct relations):** all existing dossier-persons are co-present in Mudbrook with overlapping relational ties. Melissa, Vespera, Rill, Bertram, Aldous, Copperplate form a tight Mudbrook-centered constellation. Two diegetic artifacts circulate in related but distinct orbits (DA-0001 Brinewick-published; DA-0002 Harrowgate-filed sealed-docket).

**Mosaic view:** all existing persons are heartland-region canal-circuit-stratum. The mosaic is thin — only one region-and-stratum lens operational pre-batch.

**Dense clusters:** Mudbrook contractor-and-artist-and-craft-trade cluster (highly concentrated).

**Isolated domains (monopoly windows pre-batch):** drylands, cold north highland, wet fenlands, mountain east, Brinewick, coastal south; all species clusters B/C/D; every non-contractor professional domain (magistrate, ward-inspector, crafter, clergy, scholar, stationkeeper, Ash-Seal technician, dredger-foreman, authenticator, dowager, well-keeper, physician).

**Overrepresented clusters:** canal heartland × contractor-stratum × Cluster A-or-human × living-characters × Mudbrook.

**Mosaic mirrors pre-batch:** none (too sparse). Batch introduces one intentional mirror: NCP-0003 highland clan-elder ↔ NCP-0004 Brinewick dowager — inverse-register Cluster C inheritors in opposite institutional frames.

## Niche-Occupancy Map

- **Hard-filled:** heartland cat-folk female bardic-circuit contractor (Vespera); heartland human female named-veteran contractor (Melissa).
- **Crowded:** Mudbrook canal-heartland contractor-adjacent.
- **Adjacent open:** every other heartland profession, every other town, every other species.
- **Open:** drylands / cold north / fenland / mountain east / Brinewick as insider-voiced; Cluster B / C / D; magistrate, ward-inspector, crafter, clergy, scholar, stationkeeper, Ash-Seal technician, dredger-foreman, authenticator, dowager, well-keeper, physician.

## Phase 11 Score Matrix

| NCP | WR | ND | PR | VD | OLR | AU | TF | EP | CB | OR | Aggregate |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

**Selection via max-min distance (NOT raw-total ranking):**

1. NCP-0002 (top scorer 36) picked first. Locks fenland region + Cluster B amphibian.
2. NCP-0003 picked next (max distance from C3 on region-and-cluster axes — highland + Cluster C extinct).
3. NCP-0005 picked third (max distance from {C3, C4} — mountain east + Cluster D mythic).
4. NCP-0007 picked fourth (max distance — drylands + Cluster A dryland-adapted).
5. NCP-0004 picked fifth (Brinewick + Cluster C heartland-variant; mosaic-mirror to NCP-0003).
6. NCP-0001 picked sixth (Brinewick CF-0043 Ash-Seal insider; deep institutional specialist).
7. NCP-0006 picked seventh (baseline-human anchor + heartland reform axis + cross-card constellation nexus).

Raw-total ranking would have ranked: C3 (36) > C7 (35) = C18 (35) > C5 (34) = C13 (34) > C1 (33) = C4 (33) = C6 (33) = C21 (33) > C10 (32) = C15 (32) > C2 (31) = C17 (31) > C9 (30) = C12 (30). C9 chosen at 7th over higher-ranked C7 / C18 / C13 / C10 / C15 because max-min-distance prioritized baseline-human anchor and multi-axis cross-card constellation.

**No empty slots. No lower-scoring candidate slotted to avoid empties. Rule honored.**

**Contrast axes (8 required):**
- **elite ↔ common:** NCP-0004, NCP-0003, NCP-0005, NCP-0006 elite vs NCP-0001, NCP-0002, NCP-0007 common
- **settled ↔ mobile:** NCP-0002, NCP-0003, NCP-0005, NCP-0007 settled vs NCP-0001, NCP-0006 mobile-regional
- **literate ↔ oral:** NCP-0004, NCP-0005, NCP-0006 literate vs NCP-0003, NCP-0007 oral-primary vs NCP-0001, NCP-0002 bilingual
- **orthodox ↔ heterodox:** NCP-0003 traditional vs NCP-0006 reform vs NCP-0001 dissent-covenant vs NCP-0004 old-money-orthodox
- **lawful ↔ gray:** NCP-0001 covenant-strained gray vs all others lawful
- **old ↔ mid:** NCP-0003 ~70 + NCP-0004 ~62 + NCP-0007 ~53 vs NCP-0001, NCP-0002, NCP-0005, NCP-0006 ~38-52
- **kin-tied ↔ detached:** NCP-0003, NCP-0004, NCP-0007 kin-heavy vs NCP-0001, NCP-0006 political
- **local ↔ transregional:** all regional per parameter

All 8 contrast axes covered.

| Pairwise hard-duplication within batch | C(7,2) = 21 pairs | **PASS** — no two selected cards hard-duplicate on function+world-position+pressure+access+epistemic+voice |
| Joint mystery-closure (3-fact and 2-fact coalitions across seeds) | All 20 MR entries × all 2-seed coalitions | **PASS** — no two seeds jointly close an MR entry; no seed alone crosses firewall |
| Joint registry-duplication | Each 2-seed coalition vs each registry entry | **PASS** — no 2-seed coalition converges to single registry entry's niche |
